name: Greece (Ελλάδα)
summary: Greece is a civil-law unitary republic whose family-court framework operates under the Astikos Kodikas (Civil Code) Book Four (Family Law). Parental responsibility (γονική μέριμνα / goniki merimna) is governed by AK arts. 1510-1541; joint exercise of parental responsibility was strengthened by Law 4800/2021 in force 16 September 2021, which established explicit statutory provisions on shared residence, contact-time presumptions, and contact-obstruction sanctions. The Areios Pagos (Court of Cassation, Athens) is the apex court for civil matters; family-law decisions reach Areios Pagos via the Efeteio (Court of Appeal) following first-instance Monomeles Protodikeio (District Court) determinations. Psychology profession is regulated under Law 991/1979 and the subsequent legal framework; statutory licensing operates under the Ministry of Health. Greece is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, but the Law 4800/2021 explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate engaged the PA-construct as part of its policy basis, making Greece notable within the corpus for explicit legislative reference to alienating-conduct concerns even without statutory PAS codification.

statutory_framework.citation: Astikos Kodikas arts. 1510-1541 (as amended by Law 4800/2021)
statutory_framework.title: Civil Code Book Four — Parental Responsibility
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal civil-code basis for parental responsibility. Law 4800/2021 (in force 16 September 2021) substantially reformed the AK family-law framework: strengthened joint-exercise of parental responsibility; introduced presumption of minimum 1/3 contact-time; established statutory grounds for sanctioning sustained obstruction of contact. Central provisions for any PA-adjacent custody-modification analysis.

statutory_framework.title: Νόμος 4800/2021 — Reform of family-law framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate for Law 4800/2021 explicitly engaged the parental-alienation construct as part of the policy basis for the reform. Makes Greece notable within the corpus for legislative-record engagement with PA reasoning without statutory codification of PAS as a diagnostic label.

statutory_framework.title: Psychologists Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute regulating the psychology profession. Statutory licensing under the Ministry of Health. Statutory anchor for evaluator-quality in court-appointed psychological assessments.

apex_courts.name: Areios Pagos (Court of Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Apex court of cassation for civil and criminal matters. Family-law decisions reach Areios Pagos via the Efeteio (Court of Appeal) following Monomeles Protodikeio (District Court) determinations. Operates as a cassation court reviewing legal-question determinations.

professional_regulators.name: Hellenic Psychological Society (Ελληνική Ψυχολογική Εταιρεία / ELPSE)
professional_regulators.role: Peak academic-and-professional psychology society in Greece. Operates ethics code and complaints procedure.

professional_regulators.name: Panhellenic Psychological Association (Σύλλογος Ελλήνων Ψυχολόγων / SEPS)
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional union of Greek psychologists. Operates ethics code and complaints procedure alongside Ministry-of-Health licensing under Law 991/1979.

anonymisation_convention: Greek family-law decisions are anonymised per Areios Pagos and Efeteio practice using initials. Published decisions on the Areios Pagos portal strip identifying details.

key_developments.title: Astikos Kodikas (Civil Code) enacted
key_developments.description: Greek Civil Code (Αστικός Κώδικας) enacted 1946, in force 23 February 1946 — drawing on German BGB substantive heritage (Heinrich Maximilian Pappenheim and German pandectist tradition influences). Book Four (Family Law) substantively governed Greek family law for 75+ years prior to substantial 1983 and 2021 reforms.

key_developments.title: Democratic transition + 1975 Constitution
key_developments.description: Greek democratic transition July 1974 ended seven-year military junta (1967-1974) — referendum 8 December 1974 abolished monarchy. Constitution of Greece 1975 adopted 11 June 1975 — establishing parliamentary republic framework with Art. 21 codifying family-protection and Art. 13 freedom of religion.

key_developments.title: Law 991/1979 Psychologists Act + ECHR ratification
key_developments.description: Federal statutory regulation of psychology profession enacted 21 December 1979 — among the earliest dedicated psychology-regulation statutes in EU/EEA region. Statutory licensing under Ministry of Health. Concurrently, Greece ratified ECHR 28 November 1974 effective (in addition to founding Council of Europe membership 9 August 1949).

key_developments.title: Family Law modernisation (Law 1329/1983)
key_developments.description: Substantial modernisation of Astikos Kodikas Book Four — abolition of patriarchal family-law structure; gender-equal parental responsibility; civil marriage; no-fault divorce. Major post-democratic-transition family-law reform.

key_developments.title: Greece ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Greece ratified the UNCRC on 11 May 1993. Concurrently, Greece acceded to the Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction 1980 effective 1 June 1993. Substantive cross-border family-law engagement subsequently developed.

key_developments.title: Eurozone accession + EU-acquis Brussels IIa framework
key_developments.description: Greece joined the Eurozone 1 January 2001 (EU member since 1981 — second post-Mediterranean expansion). Substantially integrated EU acquis communautaire including Brussels IIa Regulation (now Brussels IIb 2019/1111) for cross-border child-abduction and custody matters.

key_developments.title: Greek financial crisis + bailout context + family-law modernisation continued
key_developments.description: Greek government-debt crisis began 2009 with subsequent EU/IMF bailout programmes 2010-2018 — substantively constraining policy-reform trajectory including family-law domain. Substantial demographic and economic stress affecting family-court caseload.

key_developments.title: Civil Partnership Law for same-sex couples (Law 4356/2015)
key_developments.description: Civil Partnership Law (Σύμφωνο συμβίωσης) extended to same-sex couples by Law 4356/2015 enacted 24 December 2015. Same-sex marriage subsequently legalised by Law 5089/2024 enacted 15 February 2024 — first Orthodox-Christian-majority country to legalise same-sex marriage.

key_developments.title: Prespa Agreement + North Macedonia name-resolution + Hellenic-cultural-heritage framework
key_developments.description: Prespa Agreement signed 17 June 2018 between Greece and former-Yugoslav-Republic-of-Macedonia resolving 27-year name-dispute (1991-2018) + Greek ratification 25 January 2019 + North Macedonia constitutional name-change effective 12 February 2019 — substantively distinctive Balkan diplomatic-resolution framework + structurally distinctive globally only modern bilateral-treaty-resolved sustained name-dispute framework + foundational substantive North-Macedonia-EU-NATO-accession framework affecting subsequent cross-border family-law-implementation between Greece + North Macedonia + Brussels IIb Regulation framework + Hague Convention 1980 implementation framework for cross-border parental-responsibility cases.

key_developments.title: Law 4800/2021 in force — PA-construct legislative-record engagement
key_developments.description: Substantial reform of parental-responsibility framework in force 16 September 2021. Strengthened joint-exercise default; introduced presumption of minimum 1/3 contact-time; established statutory grounds for sanctioning sustained contact-obstruction. Legislative-record engagement with PA-construct reasoning in the explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate — among the rare jurisdictions globally with explicit legislative-record PA-construct engagement without statutory PAS codification.

key_developments.title: Law 4800/2021 implementation jurisprudence + Synigoros tou Politi institutional position
key_developments.description: Law 4800/2021 implementation jurisprudence development 2022-2023 — multiple Areios Pagos and Cour d'Appel decisions interpreting joint-exercise default + 1/3 contact-time presumption + contact-obstruction sanctioning framework. Synigoros tou Politi (Greek Ombudsman) Department for Children's Rights institutional position addressing PA-related issues within child-rights framework. Substantively significant institutional jurisprudential development — establishing trajectory toward 2024 Areios Pagos + same-sex marriage 2024 substantive register.

key_developments.title: Areios Pagos + same-sex marriage Law 5089/2024 effective + best-interests substantive register
key_developments.description: Areios Pagos continues to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Astikos Kodikas arts. 1510-1541 (as amended by Law 4800/2021) + Constitution Art. 21 + ECHR Article 8 framework in custody disputes including PA-construct-legislative-record-engaged framework. Same-sex marriage Law 5089/2024 effective 15 February 2024 — first Orthodox-Christian-majority country same-sex-marriage legalisation.

structural_findings: Greece sits structurally within the EU civil-law cluster — Astikos Kodikas + Law 4800/2021 reform + Areios Pagos cassation apex. Law 4800/2021 makes Greece notable for explicit legislative-record engagement with PA-construct reasoning even without statutory PAS codification — structurally distinct from Brazil (Lei 12.318/2010) and Mexico (state codes) which codified PA-related provisions explicitly.

Law 4800/2021 contact-time presumption (minimum 1/3) and obstruction-sanction framework places Greece structurally adjacent to Belgium's astreintes regime (Civil Code art. 387ter) and to the Brazilian Lei 12.318/2010 framework — without naming PA in statutory text.

Psychology profession regulation operates under Law 991/1979 + Ministry of Health licensing — less centralised than the federal-statutory regimes elsewhere in the corpus but still operates statutory title oversight.
editorial_notes: Greece jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-08 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Astikos-Kodikas-to-contemporary trajectory: 1946-Astikos-Kodikas-enacted + 1974-democratic-transition-+-1975-Constitution + 1979-Law-991-Psychologists-Act-+-ECHR-ratification + 1983-Family-Law-modernisation-Law-1329 + 1992-UNCRC-+-Hague-1980-accession + 2001-Eurozone-accession + 2009-Greek-financial-crisis + 2015-Civil-Partnership-Law-same-sex-Law-4356 + 2021-Law-4800-PA-construct-legislative-record + 2024-Areios-Pagos-+-same-sex-marriage-Law-5089-+-best-interests.

EU civil-law framework (AK + Law 4800/2021 reform + Areios Pagos cassation + Law 991/1979 psychology regulation + Constitution 1975 + Council of Europe/ECHR + Hague Convention 1980 + EU acquis + Eurozone + Civil Partnership Law 2015 + same-sex marriage Law 2024).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — but Law 4800/2021 explanatory memorandum explicitly engaged PA-construct reasoning. Structurally distinct from Brazil/Mexico explicit codification.

Joins EU-civil-law-cluster + Mediterranean-sub-cluster + Council-of-Europe-founding-member-1949 + ECHR-1974-ratification + Hague-1980-Eastern-Mediterranean + Eurozone-2001 + first-Orthodox-Christian-majority-same-sex-marriage-2024 + PA-construct-legislative-record-engagement-without-statutory-codification (distinct from Brazil Lei 12.318/2010, Mexico state codes) + German-BGB-Civil-Code-substrate + 1/3-contact-time-presumption-distinctive + obstruction-sanction-Belgian-astreintes-adjacent-framework clusters within the corpus.
